steps.title: Minimum value definition
steps.content: The minimum value of a data set is the smallest value that occurs at least once (frequency > 0).

steps.title: Table lookup
steps.content: The answer choices are 1, 6, 20, and 26. Scanning the table, neither 1 nor 6 appears as a listed value with a positive frequency — they are not entries in the data set. The smallest value that does appear with a non-zero frequency is 20. That matches choice C — C is correct.
